fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response effectiveness on the property may be achieved by addressing the single access point through expanding or designating additional entry routes, thereby improving responder access. Establishing pathways that navigate through or bypass dense vegetation could facilitate quicker movement and clearer sightlines for responders. Improving rear access to the buildings by creating unobstructed pathways or access roads may also support operational flexibility. Maintaining a clear and unobstructed front driveway would assist with responder staging and navigation, while managing vegetation around this area could enhance visibility for personnel on site. Addressing these elements could collectively support effective emergency operations and responder safety.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the separation between vegetation and the structure could improve defensible space and support long-term wildfire resilience. Managing scattered trees and shrubs, particularly those adjacent to the structure, by selectively thinning or pruning, can reduce fuel continuity and help prevent fire spread to the building. Maintaining and potentially expanding the open terrain around the property as a fuel break could provide an effective barrier to wildfire movement. Continuing to support open fuel breaks around the property boundaries might decrease the likelihood of fire crossing these areas. By focusing on these actions, the property can improve its preparedness and support firefighting efforts in the event of a wildfire.
